queryStrategy: fresh angles not yet in queue/published — emphasizing this-week launches (Cursor 2.5, May 18), new model/benchmark moves (Mythos Preview, SWE-bench Pro), protocol shifts (WebMCP, MCP roadmap), and production-grade practices (observability, cache hit rate, multi-agent patterns)

topics.title: Cursor Composer 2.5 vs Claude Code Opus 4.7 — Head-to-Head After the May 18 Launch
topics.whyTrending: Cursor Composer 2.5 shipped May 18, 2026 claiming parity with Opus 4.7 and GPT-5.5 at $0.50/M in / $2.50/M out. Hot week, comparison searches spike, no in-depth post yet.

topics.title: WebMCP — How Google's New Chrome Protocol Turns Every Website Into a Tool for AI Agents
topics.whyTrending: Google shipped WebMCP in Chrome Canary in February 2026. Turns any site into a structured MCP tool. Massive implications for browser agents and dev tooling; almost no practical write-ups yet.

topics.title: Greptile vs CodeRabbit vs Bugbot — AI Code Review Showdown Backed by Real Benchmarks
topics.whyTrending: Greptile 82% catch rate vs CodeRabbit 44% vs Bugbot 58% — fresh numbers, vendors actively fighting for market share. High commercial-intent search.

topics.title: Prompt Cache Hit Rate — The One Metric That Cuts Your Claude API Bill by 80%
topics.whyTrending: Audit of 30 production teams in Mar–May 2026 found 5–10× overspend from missing caching. Most existing posts are surface-level; nobody has built a structured monitoring playbook around the cache hit rate metric.

topics.title: Multi-Agent Systems in Production — 3 Patterns That Actually Survived 2026's Reality Check
topics.whyTrending: Five top vendors (Anthropic, OpenAI, AutoGen, Cognition, LangChain) converged on orchestrator + isolated-subagents. 'More agents = more intelligence' hype collapsed mid-2026. Post-hype practical guide is missing.

topics.title: The AI Agent Observability Stack — Langfuse vs Braintrust vs LangSmith for Production Teams
topics.whyTrending: Agents burn 50× more tokens than chat; observability moved from 'nice to have' to mandatory in 2026. Three platforms with clearly differentiated bets (OSS / eval-science / LangGraph) — perfect comparison post.

topics.title: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering — Karpathy Calls Time of Death, Now What?
topics.whyTrending: Andrej Karpathy (who coined 'vibe coding' in Feb 2025) admitted in 2026 that the era is ending in favor of agentic engineering with specs. Controversial, opinionated, perfect for engagement.

topics.title: OpenAI Codex Multi-Day Automations — Running Coding Agents That Span Hours, Days, Sessions
topics.whyTrending: Codex's headline 2026 feature is jobs that run across days and pick back up across sessions. Most direct push into persistent execution — practical tutorials are sparse.

topics.title: SWE-bench Pro Leaderboard Decoded — Why 77.8% Beats 93.9% When Picking Your AI Stack
topics.whyTrending: Verified is saturated; Pro is contamination-resistant. Claude Mythos Preview leads Pro at 77.8% (May 13, 2026), Opus 4.7 Adaptive 64.3%, GPT-5.5 58.6%. Devs are confused about which benchmark to trust.

topics.title: MCP at Scale — Streamable HTTP, .well-known Discovery, and the 97M-Download Reality
topics.whyTrending: MCP SDK downloads grew from 100K (Nov 2024) to 97M (Mar 2026). 2026 roadmap focuses on horizontal scaling, .well-known capability discovery, enterprise SSO/audit. Advanced topic, low competition.
